oneLiner: A native macOS terminal emulator (SwiftUI + embedded Ghostty) with a workspace model and CLI built for driving Claude Code sessions.
now: Git-watch redesign (ADR 0005) implemented on branch worktree-git-watch-redesign-adr: per-Session watching replaced by an app-global repo-keyed GitMonitor — dedup watchers + global concurrency lanes, split refs(always-on)/working-tree(gated) signal, visible+busy gating, cached detection, QoS .utility + PR TTL 300s. Fixes the daily-drive lag (~12k git spawns/3d, 96/min 'Not a git repo' storm, dead-worktree churn, 899 uncapped gh calls). Build + 1207 tests green; PR pending.
next: Remote-workspace phase 2: forward the IPC socket + install shell integration remotely so a remote Claude pane gets live busy/idle, auto-title, and OSC-7 pwd tracking (deferred from PR #66).
Daily-drive the shipped app (now v1.5.11) and triage regressions; small follow-up candidates: Reset Name (custom→auto), worktree-leak-on-close, Escape-vs-⌘W placeholder binding.
Validate the /tian implement harness (bumped to v1.2.0 for the session model) on a real multi-slice run: async fan-out overlap + structured handoff (ADR 0003).

milestones.title: Agent delegation (/tian implement) shipped in a release

shipped.summary: Released v1.5.11 (build 553) — notarized + stapled, Gatekeeper-accepted, Sparkle appcast live. Ships PR #93 (busy-session CPU spike fix: TimelineView-driven relayout replaced with a static rainbow fill + CALayer-driven RainbowBorder) and PR #92 (Ghostty preference overrides in tian Settings).

Killed the busy-session CPU spike: a *visible* window with a busy session ran a full SwiftUI relayout every vsync (Time Profiler on v1.5.10: 49.6 NSHostingView.layout passes/s, 19.2% of a core, 45% of process CPU in AttributeGraph). Cause was TimelineView itself — while one is on screen SwiftUI re-walks the window's whole view graph per display cycle, whatever its minimumInterval — so PR #91's occlusion gating never touched this case. BusyDotView is now a static rainbow fill (no TimelineView), RainbowBorder spins on a CABasicAnimation/CAGradientLayer (RainbowBorderLayer, backing-scale aware), and the overview's per-card 1 Hz preview timers collapse into one shared grid tick. Re-profiled same scenario (3 busy sessions + overview open) on a Debug build: 1.5 passes/s, 3.8% of a core. Full test suite green. RainbowGlow (bell) is the last TimelineView — follow-up.

Ghostty preferences are now overridable from tian Settings (⌘, → Terminal): an Option-key picker (fixes Claude Code's alt+p, which macOS otherwise composes into Unicode) plus a free-form config box, rendered into a tian-owned file loaded after ~/.config/ghostty/config so tian's UI wins while "Default" emits no line and falls through. Applying reloads the config, which Ghostty pushes to every live surface — open panes update with no relaunch. Also fixes ⌘, itself: Ghostty binds cmd+, to open_config and the surface claimed the key equivalent before the menu bar, so Settings never opened from a pane. 18 tests green, five of them running the rendered file through the real Ghostty parser (line parses, tian's file beats a conflicting user config, Default leaves the user's value in charge, bad line yields a diagnostic); verified by hand in the app.
